Chapter 380 of the Local Government
Code authorizes municipalities to offer
incentives designed to promote economic
development such as commercial and
retail projects. Specifically, it provides for
offering loans and grants of city funds or
services at little or no cost to promote
state and local economic development
and to stimulate business and commercial
activity. Local governments set the terms
and conditions of the agreements,
including the duration. House Bill 2404
from the 87th regular legislative session
requires local governments to report these
agreements to the Comptroller, through
an online reporting tool. The agreement
documents must be available to the public
by the Comptroller while the agreement is
active.
